title: Session publishing control
description: Give users agency over when sessions are published to the ledger. Today, sessions auto-upload on stop and orphaned sessions auto-recover+upload. Users need: (1) ability to abort/discard sessions, (2) confirmation before uploading recovered sessions, (3) future: config for publish mode (auto/manual).

title: Fix hooks check-vs-install mismatch (issue #34)
description: ensureClaudeHooks checks user-level settings.json but installs to project-level settings.local.json. Fix all four issues: wrong check file, incomplete hook detection, ClaudeAgent.List() wrong file, and add doctor --fix for stale hooks.
close_reason: Fixed ensureClaudeHooks to check project-level settings, made HasProjectClaudeHooks verify both events, fixed ClaudeAgent.List(), added doctor hook completeness check with --fix support

title: Research: should daemon start as part of ox init?
description: After seeing test results, investigate: Could ox init call autoStartDaemon() + RequestSync()? Should doctor detect just-initialized state and suppress warnings? Review IPC architecture constraints. Compare tradeoffs (latency vs UX cleanliness).
close_reason: Research complete. IPC architecture doc (line 29) already specifies init should start daemon. Current code only requests sync if daemon already running. Three issues found: (1) git repo paths fails because team context dir not cloned, (2) .sageox/ changes warning because files uncommitted, (3) uncommitted changes warning. Fix: add autoStartDaemon() + RequestSync() to init, OR make doctor's git repo paths check tolerate not-yet-synced state.

title: Extract findRepoTeamContext helper in config package
description: Create a helper function findRepoTeamContext(projectRoot) that loads ProjectConfig.TeamID and LocalConfig.TeamContexts, finds the TC matching the repo's team, and falls back to [0]. This is the shared logic used by status, team-ctx, and prime. Place in internal/config/local_config.go or a new file.
close_reason: Added FindRepoTeamContext and IsRepoTeamContext helpers to internal/config/local_config.go

title: Add 5th prompt variant to attribution-guidance and subagent-discovery
description: CodeRabbit review on PR #41 noted that attribution-guidance and subagent-discovery each have 4 Texts entries while iterationsPerQuery=5. This means variant 0 runs twice (4%4==0), giving it double weight. Add a 5th phrasing to each for uniform coverage.

title: Add --name flag to session plan command
description: Add --name flag to 'ox agent <id> session plan'. Named plans: same slug overwrites (revision), new slug creates new file. Stored as plan-<slug>.md. No --name: auto-increments (plan-1.md, plan-2.md). --update flag: revises most recent plan regardless of naming. Agent picks name at capture time since it has full context. session stop ships all plans as-is, no renaming.
close_reason: scope simplified: one plan per session, no --name flag needed, guidance moves to CLI output

title: [EPIC] Post-onboarding UX gaps from user feedback
description: Rob (new user) ran ox init, had hooks installed, daemon running, ox status normal — but couldn't tell if anything was working when he opened Claude Code. This epic tracks the remaining UX gaps after recent fixes (guidance field, team context discovery, hook completeness checking). Gaps 2-6 from the audit.
close_reason: All 5 subtasks completed

title: Update doctor to suggest abort for orphaned sessions

Update agent doctor (buildAgentDoctorOutput) to detect orphaned recordings >24h old and surface both options in next_steps. Files: internal/doctor/session.go, cmd/ox/agent_doctor.go.

title: Fix sageox-sync: vague phrasings fail (2/5 benchmark failures)
description: Benchmark shows 3/5 success for sageox-sync. Failing variants: 'Is the team context up to date?' (agent checks ~/.claude/teams/ instead of ox status) and 'Are we seeing the latest team discussions or is the sync stale?' (agent runs bd sync/bd list — confusing beads sync with ox sync). Root cause: (1) Agent doesn't associate 'up to date' or 'stale' with ox sync commands (2) The word 'sync' in the prompt triggers beads sync instead of ox sync. Fix: prime output should explicitly map sync-related concepts to 'ox status' or 'ox sync' commands.
close_reason: Added top-level guidance field to agentPrimeOutput with intent-to-command mappings

title: fix: daemon persistLedgerPath writes empty path due to stale config cache
description:
## Symptom

`ox daemon status` shows ledger as 'synced' but `ox status` shows 'not cloned' for the same ledger path.

Observed on ~/Documents/Code/sageox/sageox-init where config.local.toml had:
```toml
[ledger]
path = ''
last_sync = 2026-02-18T06:10:41.576431Z
```

The daemon was syncing correctly (updating last_sync), but the path was empty — causing ox status to report 'not cloned'.

## Root Cause

Race condition between `persistLedgerPath()` and `UpdateConfigLastSync()` in the daemon. They use different config objects:

1. `persistLedgerPath()` loads a **fresh** config from disk via `config.LoadLocalConfig()`, writes `Path = '/correct/path'`, and saves to disk.
2. `UpdateConfigLastSync()` uses the workspace registry's in-memory `localConfigCache`, which still has `Ledger.Path == ''`.
3. When `UpdateConfigLastSync()` saves the stale cache to disk, it **overwrites** the path that `persistLedgerPath()` just wrote.

The sequence:
- Daemon starts, `localConfigCache` loaded with `Ledger == nil`
- `UpdateConfigLastSync('ledger')` creates `LedgerConfig{}` with empty Path in cache, saves to disk → `path = ''` in TOML
- `persistLedgerPath()` loads fresh config from disk, sees `Path == ''`, writes correct path to disk
- Next sync: `UpdateConfigLastSync('ledger')` saves cache (still has `Path == ''`) → overwrites the correct path

## Code Paths

**Daemon (works):** `WorkspaceRegistry.rebuildFromConfigLocked()` skips empty path, falls through to `InitializeLedger()` which computes the default path via `config.DefaultLedgerPath()`. Daemon knows the real path.

**ox status (broken):** `cmd/ox/status.go:1409` checks `localCfg.Ledger == nil` but NOT `localCfg.Ledger.Path == ''`. When the struct exists with empty path, the fallback to `ledger.DefaultPath()` never fires, and the `hasLedger` check at line 535 (`Path \!= ''`) is false → shows 'not cloned'.

## Suggested Fixes

### Fix 1: Root cause — persistLedgerPath should use the registry cache
Add `PersistLedgerPath(path)` method to `WorkspaceRegistry` that updates the `localConfigCache` directly, keeping it in sync with `UpdateConfigLastSync()`. Change `persistLedgerPath()` to call this instead of loading a separate config copy.

### Fix 2: Defensive — ox status should handle empty path
Change `cmd/ox/status.go:1409` from `localCfg.Ledger == nil` to `localCfg.Ledger == nil || localCfg.Ledger.Path == ''` so it falls back to default path discovery.

### Fix 3: Remove unused import
After fix 1, `internal/daemon/sync.go` no longer needs the `config` package import.

## Files Changed

- `internal/daemon/workspace_registry.go` — add `PersistLedgerPath()` method
- `internal/daemon/sync.go` — simplify `persistLedgerPath()`, remove unused config import
- `cmd/ox/status.go` — defensive empty-path fallback

title: Fix daemon sync test isolation: tests leak into real user state
description:
Three daemon sync tests fail under -race because they pick up real ~/.local/share/sageox/ team contexts instead of isolating to temp dirs. Pre-existing, not caused by any feature branch.

Failing tests (all in internal/daemon/sync_test.go):
1. TestSyncScheduler_TeamContextIntegration — expects 1 team context, gets 4 (real user teams leak in)
2. TestSyncScheduler_TeamContextMultiple — expects 2, gets 5 (same root cause)
3. TestSyncScheduler_PullTeamContext_FetchHeadDeduplication — credential injection into temp bare repo URL fails (oauth2 token injected into file:// path)

Root cause: SyncScheduler discovers team contexts from real user config/credentials on disk rather than test-scoped fixtures. Tests need to override the team context discovery path (e.g., XDG_DATA_HOME or equivalent) to point at a temp directory.

Repro: go test -count=1 -race -run 'TestSyncScheduler_PullTeamContext_FetchHeadDeduplication|TestSyncScheduler_TeamContextIntegration|TestSyncScheduler_TeamContextMultiple' ./internal/daemon/

title: Add fast version check to ox agent prime output
description: ox agent prime is the most frequently run command by AI coworkers but doesn't surface available updates. Add a non-blocking version check that tells users/agents when a newer ox version is available. Must not slow down prime - use ~100ms timeout or async/cached mechanism. Reference: ox doctor already checks GitHub releases API in cmd/ox/doctor_ecosystem.go (checkForUpdates). Server-driven version check exists in internal/api/version_check.go.

title: Consolidate SessionMeta construction into shared builder
description:
There are 4 sites constructing lfs.SessionMeta inline with no shared code. This makes adding new fields (like user_id, repo_id) a 4-file change and risks drift between sites.

## Current Sites
1. cmd/ox/agent_session.go:829 — main session stop (richest: Model, Title, Summary)
2. cmd/ox/agent_session_recover.go:174 — recovery (subset: no Model, Title, Summary)
3. cmd/ox/session_upload_cmd.go:128 — manual upload (has its own buildSessionMeta helper)
4. cmd/ox/doctor_session_upload_retry.go:261 — doctor retry (copies from orphan.Meta)

## Proposed Approach
Add a builder/constructor to internal/lfs/meta.go that all sites use. Options:
- Functional options pattern: lfs.NewSessionMeta(name, opts...)
- Builder with required + optional fields
- Simple constructor with commonly-shared fields + setters for optional ones

The key insight: sites 1 and 2 both have agent state, site 4 copies from existing meta, site 3 reconstructs from dir name. A constructor with required fields (Version, SessionName, Username, AgentID, AgentType, CreatedAt, Files) plus chainable setters for optional fields (Model, Title, Summary, EntryCount) would cover all cases cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single construction path for SessionMeta
- All 4 sites use the shared builder
- No behavioral change — existing meta.json output stays identical
- Adding a new field (like user_id) only requires changing the builder, not 4 files

title: Add session cancel/discard option to ox agent session
description: Currently ox agent session stop is the only way to end a recording, and it always processes and uploads. There's no way to cancel/discard a session (e.g., throwaway exploration, accidental start, or a session that went sideways). Add a cancel/discard mechanism - either a separate command (ox agent session cancel) or a flag on stop (ox agent session stop --discard). Should clean up the recording state file without processing or uploading.
close_reason: implemented as 'ox agent <id> session abort --force' in ox-dkw

Add 'is_repo_team bool' field to teamContextInfo JSON output so agents have explicit signal this is THE context for this repo.

Use projectCfg.TeamID to match against cloudTeamContexts[].StableID() and repoDetail.TeamContexts[].TeamID.

title: Add user_id to raw session JSONL metadata header
description:
The session.SessionMeta struct (internal/session/metadata.go) — the first line of each raw JSONL session file — is missing a user_id field. It has ox_username (email) but not the unique user ID from auth.

## Current State
- session.SessionMeta has: ox_version, ox_username (email), schema_version, agent_type, agent_version, model, session_id, oxsid, started_at, ended_at, project_remote
- Missing: user_id (from auth.StoredToken.UserInfo.UserID)

## Changes Required
1. Add UserID field to session.SessionMeta struct (omitempty for backward compat)
2. Update NewSessionMeta() and NewSessionMetaWithVersion() in internal/session/metadata.go to populate it from auth token (similar to how getOxUsername already retrieves the token)
3. No repo_id needed here — that's a ledger-level concept, and project_remote already serves this purpose in the JSONL header

## Where to Get Value
- user_id: auth.GetTokenForEndpoint(ep).UserInfo.UserID (token already fetched in getOxUsername)

## Notes
- omitempty for backward compatibility
- json tag: user_id

After Phase 3, only templates.go CSS remains. Verify it is the single source. For dev tool HTML files (~100KB), readability wins over minification.

title: Session recording captures pre-start content and truncates at plan exit
description:
## Bug Summary

Session recording for 2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO has three issues:

1. **Pre-session content included**: raw.jsonl starts at seq=0 with "switch to a new branch" — 3 entries BEFORE /ox-session-start was called (seq=3). The recording should only include content from AFTER ox agent session start completes.

2. **Session truncated at plan mode exit**: Recording ends at seq=32 "[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" which corresponds to ExitPlanMode. The entire implementation phase is missing: parallel subagent execution (3 agents), code changes to 4 files, build/lint/test verification, beads issue management, git commit, git push, and PR creation. This is ~80% of the session value.

3. **Plan content not captured**: The plan was written to a file via the Write tool but the plan content itself doesn't appear in raw.jsonl (tool entries show [0 chars]). Similarly, all subagent results are absent.

## Expected Behavior

- raw.jsonl should NOT include any entries from before ox agent session start completes
- raw.jsonl should include ALL entries from session start up to (but not including) ox agent session stop  
- Tool results (especially Write, Task subagent results) should have their content captured, not [0 chars]

## Observed Behavior

- 33 entries in raw.jsonl, 3 of which predate session start
- Recording stops at ExitPlanMode (seq=32), missing the entire implementation
- Most tool results show [0 chars] content

## Reproduction

Session: 2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO
Ledger path: ledger/sessions/2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO/
Local cache: Library/Caches/sageox/sessions/repo_019c5812.../sessions/2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO/

## Investigation Starting Points

- How does the session recorder hook into Claude Code's message stream?
- Is there a message buffer that gets flushed at session start (explaining pre-start content)?
- What causes the recording to stop mid-session? Is there a max entry count, a context compaction event, or does ExitPlanMode somehow signal an end?
- Why are tool results empty ([0 chars])? Is this intentional filtering or a bug in content extraction?

title: Make doctor warnings actionable for non-technical users
description: User feedback: 'I am not sure how to fix the warning about cloud doctor, or adding AGENTS.md at the team level.' Doctor output shows warnings like 'Team twnstr-team: AGENTS.md (missing)' with a raw file path but no clear next step for a user who doesn't know what AGENTS.md is or why it matters. Similarly, 'Cloud doctor (unavailable)' gives no guidance. Fix: Add brief remediation hints to doctor warnings. For AGENTS.md missing: explain what it does and offer to create a starter template (ox doctor --fix). For cloud doctor unavailable: explain this is normal if the cloud service is temporarily unreachable and is non-critical. Key files: cmd/ox/doctor_hooks.go, doctor check registration.

title: Add permission prompt and context compression patterns to classifyTextContent
close_reason: added context compaction, local-command-stdout, and local-command-caveat patterns

title: [HUMAN] Move /api/v1/public/repos/{repo_id}/doctor behind auth
description: The cloud doctor endpoint is currently unauthenticated under /api/v1/public/repos/. It returns repo health info (registration status, pending merges, team issues) which should not be publicly accessible. Move to an authenticated path (e.g. /api/v1/repos/{repo_id}/doctor with Bearer token). CLI already sends auth token — only server routing needs to change. Once moved, update repoDoctorPath in internal/api/repo.go to match the new path.

title: ox session view --html does not render imported JSONL entries
description:
Two sub-issues in viewHTMLBuildMessageView (session_view_html.go):

1. [FIXED in accra] Root-level content not rendered — imported JSONL has content at entry["content"], not nested under entry["data"]["content"]. Fixed by adding fallback at lines 332-337.

2. [OPEN] viewHTMLMapEntryType doesn't recognize 'user' or 'assistant' entry types — imported JSONL uses type='user' and type='assistant' at root level. The function only maps 'message'→assistant, so both fall to default→'info'. Fix: add 'user' and 'assistant' cases to match session_html.go:mapEntryType (line 401). This causes: all entries show as 'Info' badge, all collapsed by default, stats show '0 USER MESSAGES'.
notes: Root cause for missing username: planHistoryMeta struct (agent_session_plan_history.go:33) has no Username field, so _meta.username is silently dropped during JSON unmarshal. writePlanHistoryRaw (line 388) never writes it to the raw.jsonl header. Fix: add Username field to planHistoryMeta struct and carry it through to header metadata in writePlanHistoryRaw.
close_reason: Also fixed viewHTMLMapEntryType to handle user/assistant/tool entry types matching mapEntryType

title: Fix session-history v0: direct phrasing fails
description: Benchmark shows 4/5 success for session-history but v0 'Show me the session history for this project' failed — agent only did 'ls -la' (1 tool call) and stopped. The other 4 indirect phrasings all succeeded (the agent tried git log or ox session). The most direct, obvious phrasing is the one that fails. Investigate whether prime output should include a stronger hint for 'ox session list' or whether the validator needs to accept 'ls' as a first exploratory step.
close_reason: Added top-level guidance field to agentPrimeOutput with intent-to-command mappings

title: Fix identified fresh install issues
description: Based on findings from E2E tests and daemon research, implement fixes. Options include: starting daemon in init, making doctor bootstrapping-aware based on init timestamp, or combination. Ensure ox init + ox doctor flow produces zero unexpected warnings.
close_reason: Both fixes implemented: (A) init now auto-starts daemon and requests sync, (B) doctor git repo paths check tolerates recently-initialized state. E2E test passes with 0 failures, 0 unexpected warnings.

title: Session push gets 403 from git.sageox.ai despite valid PAT
description: git push to ledger repo fails with HTTP 403 from git.sageox.ai (via AWS ALB). The PAT embedded in the remote URL matches the stored credentials and expires 2027-02-11. git pull works fine (daemon syncs successfully). The PAT may lack write_repository scope, or the server has a push restriction. Error: 'RPC failed; HTTP 403 curl 22 The requested URL returned error: 403'. Also 'failed to store: -25300' macOS keychain error (side effect).

title: Extract shared MapEntryType to internal/session/
description: Entry type mapping (user->human, assistant->assistant, tool/tool_result->tool) is duplicated 5 times across: generator.go, session_html.go, session_view_html.go, markdown.go, session_view.go. Create a single MapEntryType(raw string) (cssClass, label string) in internal/session/ that returns both the CSS class and display label. Update all 5 call sites.
close_reason: Created shared MapEntryType and MapRoleToType in internal/session/entry.go, updated normalizeMessageType in generator.go to delegate to shared mapper

title: Improve prime output for benchmark failures
description: Epic: Prime benchmark revealed 8/40 failures across team-discussions (0/5), session-history (1/5), and sageox-sync (2/5). Root cause is prime output not guiding agents strongly enough toward the right commands. This epic tracks all improvements needed.

title: Add session abort command and doctor recovery confirmation
close_reason: implemented session abort command and doctor recovery confirmation

title: Add user_id and repo_id to LFS session meta.json
description:
The lfs.SessionMeta struct (internal/lfs/meta.go) is missing user_id and repo_id fields. These should be included in meta.json when available, for provenance and analytics.

## Current State
- lfs.SessionMeta has: version, session_name, username (email), agent_id, agent_type, model, title, created_at, entry_count, summary, files
- Missing: user_id (from auth.StoredToken.UserInfo.UserID) and repo_id (from ledger.Ledger.RepoID or project config)

## Changes Required
1. Add UserID and RepoID fields to lfs.SessionMeta struct (omitempty so existing meta.json files remain compatible)
2. Update all 4 construction sites to populate these fields:
   - cmd/ox/agent_session.go:829 (main session stop)
   - cmd/ox/agent_session_recover.go:174 (session recovery)
   - cmd/ox/session_upload_cmd.go:155 (manual upload)
   - cmd/ox/doctor_session_upload_retry.go:261 (doctor retry)
3. Update tests in internal/lfs/meta_test.go

## Where to Get Values
- user_id: auth.GetTokenForEndpoint(ep).UserInfo.UserID (already available via getAuthenticatedUsername pattern)
- repo_id: Need to resolve from ledger or project config. The ledger.Ledger struct has RepoID. Also available from config.LoadProjectContext(root).

## Notes
- Fields must be omitempty for backward compatibility with existing sessions
- json tags: user_id, repo_id (snake_case to match existing convention)

title: Reorder init next-steps and fix uncertain wording

Also ox doctor is step 4 (after inviting teammates) but verification should come before social actions. Fix: (1) Change 'should auto-run' to 'will auto-run'. (2) Move ox doctor to step 2 (right after commit/push). (3) Combine 'Start Claude Code' and 'auto-run prime' into one step: 'Start Claude Code — ox will auto-prime and surface your team context'. File: cmd/ox/init.go:760-770

title: Update Makefile benchmark cost/time comment

title: Extract shared ParseTimestamp to internal/session/
description: Timestamp parsing (RFC3339Nano/RFC3339 with fallback, format to 15:04:05) is duplicated 4 times. Create a single ParseTimestamp(ts string) (time.Time, bool) and FormatEntryTimestamp(ts string) string in internal/session/. Replace inline parsing in: generator.go (formatTimestamp), session_html.go (htmlEntryFromJSONL), session_view_html.go (viewHTMLEntryFromJSONL), markdown.go (mdExtractTimestamp + inline).
close_reason: Created shared ParseTimestamp and ExtractEntryTimestamp in internal/session/entry.go, updated generator.go and removed inline timestamp parsing

title: Add golden-file regression tests for JSONL→HTML/Markdown rendering
description:
No regression test exists for the session rendering bugs fixed in ox-aql. Create:

1. A canonical test fixture (testdata/imported_session.jsonl) that exercises ALL format variants:
   - _meta header with username, agent_type, ox_version, started_at
   - Root-level content (imported JSONL format)
   - Nested data.content (standard recording format)
   - ts field (imported) AND timestamp field (standard)
   - user/assistant/tool/system entry types at root level
   - message entry type with data.role (legacy nested format)

2. Golden-file tests that run this fixture through ALL output paths and assert:
   - Username appears in HTML metadata AND per-message sender labels
   - Agent type appears correctly
   - ox_version appears in header metadata
   - All entry types render with correct CSS classes (user/assistant/tool/system)
   - Root-level content renders (not empty)
   - Nested content renders (not empty)
   - Both ts and timestamp fields parse correctly
   - Output filename is session.html (not raw.html)
   - Markdown output contains entry content with correct role headers

3. Paths to test:
   - Path 1: internal/session/html/generator.go (GenerateToFile)
   - Path 2: cmd/ox/session_html.go (generateHTML)  
   - Path 3: cmd/ox/session_view_html.go (viewHTMLGenerate)
   - Markdown: internal/session/markdown.go (GenerateToFile)

This MUST be completed BEFORE the refactoring epic ox-iz9 begins, to prevent regressions.
close_reason: Implemented 41 regression tests across all 4 rendering paths (Path 1: generator, Path 2: generateHTML, Path 3: viewHTML, Markdown) covering imported and standard session formats. Tests validate: metadata (username, agent type, model, ox_version), root-level and nested content rendering, entry type mapping, sender labels, timestamp parsing (ts and timestamp fields), duration computation, tool call details, no per-entry timestamps, cross-path consistency, file generation, and ReadSessionFromPath integration.

title: Fix Path 1 sender labels — capitalize Human/Assistant
description: generator.go sets Sender: e.Type which shows lowercase 'user' and 'assistant' in HTML. Should show capitalized 'Human' and 'Assistant' like Paths 2/3 do. Use the shared MapEntryType function from Phase 2.
close_reason: Already fixed — generator.go convertEntry uses capitalized defaults (User/Assistant) and metadata-derived labels (username/formatAgentName)

title: Auto session recording default + /clear lifecycle
description: Change session recording default from manual to auto. Add --source flag to prime for /clear detection. Stop session on /clear, start new if auto, notify if manual. Enhanced transparency notices.
close_reason: Implemented auto session recording default, --source flag for clear lifecycle, and enhanced transparency notices

title: Improve Amp agent detection and accuracy (#43)
description: Fix Amp detection (add AMP_THREAD_URL), correct URL/config path, update capabilities, add tests
close_reason: Improved Amp detection (AMP_THREAD_URL), fixed URL/config path, updated capabilities, added tests

title: Add session abort command
description: Implement 'ox agent <id> session abort --force' to discard the active session without uploading to ledger. Clears .recording.json, removes session cache folder. Requires --force flag (agents confirm with user first). Includes skill files for /ox-session-abort. Files: cmd/ox/agent_session_abort.go, cmd/ox/agent.go (routing), .claude/commands/ox-session-abort.md, extensions/claude/commands/ox-session-abort.md.
close_reason: implemented in this session

title: Early write-access check on session start for public repo viewers
description: When a non-member tries to record a session on a public repo, session start succeeds but session stop fails with a confusing timeout during LFS upload. Add checkUploadAccess() call during runAgentSessionStart() to detect read-only access upfront and refuse to record with a clear error message.

title: ox doctor --fix should auto-sync ledger when ahead of remote
description: Ledger branch status and synced with remote checks are both FixLevelCheckOnly but should be FixLevelSuggested. When --fix is passed, doctor should run the equivalent of ox sync to push local commits to remote. Currently just tells user to run ox sync manually.
close_reason: Implemented: changed both checkLedgerBranchStatus and checkLedgerCleanWorkdir from FixLevelCheckOnly to FixLevelAuto. Auto-pushes when ahead, auto-pulls when behind, auto-rebase+push when diverged, auto-commits dirty workdir. Build passes, lint clean.

title: Remove dead code: mdExtractTimestamp, processContentWithMermaid
description: Dead functions to remove: 1) mdExtractTimestamp in markdown.go — defined but never called (RenderMarkdown does inline parsing instead). 2) processContentWithMermaid in session_view_html.go — defined but never called. Clean up any other dead code found during consolidation.
close_reason: Removed dead mdExtractTimestamp from markdown.go and processContentWithMermaid from session_view_html.go along with orphaned tests

title: Fix team-discussions: agent never runs ox agent team-ctx (0/5 benchmark)
description: Benchmark shows 0/5 success for team-discussions across all 5 prompt variants. The agent reads AGENTS.md (which lists the command) but never actually runs 'ox agent team-ctx'. Instead it tries git log, ls, Glob, ox status — everything except the right command. Root cause: prime output lists team-ctx as an 'available command' but doesn't instruct the agent to USE it when asked about team discussions. Fix options: (1) Add a stronger hint in prime output like 'To access team discussions, run: ox agent team-ctx' (2) Add a suggested_commands field to prime output (3) Make the AGENTS.md fixture more directive. This is the biggest benchmark gap — team discussion retrieval is a core ox value prop.
close_reason: Added top-level guidance field to agentPrimeOutput with intent-to-command mappings

title: Add team context readiness state to prime output
description: Gap 5: When team context directory doesn't exist yet (daemon still syncing), prime returns team_context: null with no hint it will appear soon. Agent has no way to tell user 'check back in a moment.' Fix: When team context path is computed but directory is empty/missing, include a team_context_status: 'syncing' field so agents can say 'Team context is syncing, will be available shortly.' File: cmd/ox/agent_prime.go (team context discovery section)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add tests for session start read-only access check
description: Add tests verifying: (1) checkUploadAccess returning ErrReadOnly → session start returns error with clear message, (2) checkUploadAccess returning nil → session start proceeds normally, (3) checkUploadAccess returning other errors → session start proceeds (fail-open). Follow existing test patterns in cmd/ox/agent_session_test.go.

title: Fresh install UX: init then doctor should not show warnings
description: Epic: Users doing ox init on an existing repo immediately see ox doctor issues. Build E2E test framework, capture what breaks, research daemon-in-init, fix issues.
close_reason: Epic complete. E2E test framework built, issues identified and fixed: init starts daemon, doctor graceful about bootstrapping.

title: Add ox agent <id> session delete command
description: Add a new 'session delete' subcommand that deletes an existing (already stopped) session from the ledger. Unlike 'session abort' which discards an active recording, 'session delete' removes a previously completed session. Should support --force flag for non-interactive use (registered on agentCmd, same pattern as abort). Needs confirmation prompt for interactive terminals.

title: Filter tool noise from session summarization
description: Issue #26: Sessions capture too much exploratory tool call noise. Add FilterForSummarization() to strip read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep) and noise bash commands before sending to LLM summarizer. Keep raw.jsonl complete for auditability.
close_reason: Implemented FilterForSummarization() in summarize.go, applied to buildSummarizeRequest() and resummary prompt. Filters read-only tools and noise bash commands while preserving errors and state-changing operations.

title: Add GenerateFromJSONL to internal/session/html generator
description: Path 1 generator currently only accepts parsed *session.Session. Add GenerateFromJSONL(lines []string, title string, opts ...Option) method that accepts raw JSONL and returns HTML. Support options: WithSummary(html), WithCompanionSummary(path). This bridges the gap so export/view/push-summary can use the canonical generator.
close_reason: Not needed — generator already has Generate/GenerateToFile/GenerateWithSummary accepting *StoredSession. All callers parse JSONL via ReadSessionFromPath before calling. GenerateFromJSONL would be a trivial wrapper with no callers.

title: parseLine drops all but first content block of assistant messages
Remove deprecated non-endpoint-aware checkout functions

title: Surface daemon sync timeout during init
description: Gap 4: If daemon doesn't become healthy within 2 seconds during init, it logs to slog.Debug and moves on silently. User gets no indication sync is pending/delayed. The conditional only shows 'Run ox sync' if sync completely failed, but the 2s timeout case is silently ignored. Fix: When daemon sync times out, show a brief note: 'Background sync starting... run ox status to check progress.' File: cmd/ox/init.go:672-707
close_reason: Closed

title: [EPIC] Consolidate session HTML/Markdown generation
description:
Consolidate three diverged HTML rendering pipelines and duplicated markdown generation into a single, well-tested pipeline in internal/session/html/. Currently there are 5 copies of entry type mapping, 4 copies of timestamp parsing, 3 copies of HTML escaping, 3 copies of CSS/JS, and 5 dead functions. See ox-uo2 for initial analysis.

Winner: Path 1 (internal/session/html/) — proper package, Go html/template, typed data model, has tests.

Four phases:
1. Eliminate same-package duplicates in cmd/ox/ (~120 lines removed, zero risk)
2. Extract shared JSONL parsing to internal/session/
3. Converge cmd/ox HTML generation onto Path 1 generator
4. CSS convergence to single readable source

title: LFS upload: include all plan files
description: Update session_upload.go content file list to include plan-*.md pattern so all named plans are uploaded to the ledger.
close_reason: scope simplified: one plan per session, no --name flag needed, guidance moves to CLI output

title: Add per-step timing instrumentation to ox agent prime
description: ox agent prime has no latency metrics. When it runs slow (e.g., 25s observed), there's no way to diagnose which step was slow. Add slog.Debug timing for each phase of runAgentPrime (daemon health check, session marker reads, auth check, instance store ops, session recording, team context discovery, guidance load, daemon attachment). Include total elapsed time in JSON output. Friction telemetry captures errors but not latency - this fills that gap.

title: Prime benchmark: multi-phrasing + coverage gaps
description: Add 5 prompt variants per query, replace arch-decisions with project-guidance, add attribution-guidance and subagent-discovery queries, tighten validators, bump iterations to 5
close_reason: Implemented multi-phrasing (5 variants per query), replaced arch-decisions with project-guidance, added attribution-guidance and subagent-discovery queries, tightened validators, bumped iterations to 5. All tests pass.

title: Config setting for session publish mode (auto/manual)
description: Add a config setting 'session.publishing' with values 'auto' (default, current behavior) and 'manual'. In manual mode, session stop saves locally but does not push to ledger. User explicitly pushes with 'ox session upload' or similar. This gives full control over what gets shared. Config location: .sageox/config.json or user config. Future work - keep it simple for now.

title: session import should generate session.html and upload all LFS artifacts
description: The import pipeline only uploads raw.jsonl to LFS. Normal session stop also generates and uploads session.html, summary.md, and session.md. The website serves session.html at /repo/.../sessions/.../view so imported sessions have no viewable HTML on the web. Fix: import should run the same HTML generation and LFS upload that session stop does.
close_reason: Import pipeline now generates session.html, session.md, and summary.md alongside raw.jsonl. These match the artifacts session stop produces, enabling the upload pipeline and web viewer to work with imported sessions.

title: Clone team contexts with --single-branch --branch main
description: Currently all git clone operations fetch all branches. For team contexts we only need main. Add --single-branch --branch main to team-context clones in: (1) daemon Checkout in sync.go:1523, (2) gitserver CheckoutOptions in checkout.go, (3) doctor fallback in doctor_git_repos.go:1557. Ledger clones unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented --single-branch --branch main for team context clones in daemon, gitserver, and doctor fallback paths

description: Daemon conflates token validity with team membership discovery. New teams invisible until token nears expiry. Fix: add discoverTeams() that re-fetches repo list on separate 5-min cadence. GH issue #39.

title: Replace openBrowser with cli.OpenInBrowser

title: Add checkUploadAccess call in runAgentSessionStart
description: In cmd/ox/agent_session.go, insert checkUploadAccess(projectRoot) call after the auth check (line 74) and before the terms notice (line 77). If errors.Is(err, api.ErrReadOnly), return a clear error: 'you have read-only access to this repo — sessions cannot be uploaded to the ledger'. This is fail-open by design: checkUploadAccess returns nil on network errors, missing auth, or API failures. Only explicit ErrReadOnly blocks recording. Reference: checkUploadAccess already exists in cmd/ox/session_upload.go lines 23-55.

Normalize team-context repo type string

The IPC struct comment says 'team_context' so standardize on that. Check all callers of cloneInBackground and Checkout in sync.go (lines 1677, 1776) and doctor_git_repos.go (line 1427).

This would make recovery ask: 'Found orphaned session from X. Upload to ledger? [y/N]' and if no, offer to discard. Relevant files: cmd/ox/agent_session_recover.go, internal/doctor/session.go.

close_reason: Report captured. Findings: 1 failure (git repo paths - team context dir not found), 2 unexpected warnings (.sageox/ unstaged, 17 uncommitted changes). Root cause: init creates files but daemon not running to clone team contexts, and user hasn't committed yet.

After ox init, running ox doctor should reassure user that hooks, daemon, and team context are all healthy. Fix: Add a summary line at end of ox doctor when all checks pass, e.g. 'All checks passed. Hooks: active | Daemon: running | Team context: synced'. Files: cmd/ox/doctor.go or cmd/ox/doctor_hooks.go

title: Add .sageox/ symlinks for ledger and teams
description: Create symlinks: .sageox/ledger -> user dir ledger, .sageox/teams/<team_id> -> user dir team, .sageox/teams/primary -> primary team. Update .gitignore with ledger and teams/ entries. ox doctor should auto-fix missing gitignore entries.
close_reason: Added CreateProjectLedgerSymlink, CreateProjectTeamSymlinks (with primary alias). Updated sageoxGitignoreContent and requiredGitignoreEntries with ledger/teams/ entries.

title: Delete loadViewSummaryHTML duplicate
description: session_view.go:loadViewSummaryHTML is identical to session_export.go:loadSummaryHTML. Delete the duplicate, call the original. Same package, zero risk.
close_reason: loadViewSummaryHTML does not exist in codebase — already removed or never implemented

title: Reduce sageox-issues tool calls: agent takes 3-5 calls to find ox doctor
description: Benchmark shows 5/5 success but median 5 tool calls before finding ox doctor. The agent explores (ls, Read, Glob) before reaching for the right command. Prime output should make 'ox doctor' more prominent as THE health check command. Consider: (1) Adding 'For health checks, run: ox doctor' to prime output (2) Making ox doctor the first suggested command when issues are suspected. Currently the agent wastes 2-4 calls exploring before finding the answer.
close_reason: Added top-level guidance field to agentPrimeOutput with intent-to-command mappings

title: Replace FETCH_HEAD mtime dedup with git ls-remote SHA compare
description: Replace time-based FETCH_HEAD mtime heuristic with definitive git ls-remote SHA comparison. When the daemon polls, first run 'git ls-remote --heads origin main' and compare to the last-known local SHA. If unchanged, skip fetch+pull entirely. This eliminates unnecessary git-upload-pack negotiations (~50% of current traffic based on production data). Applies to both ledger sync (doPull) and team context sync (pullTeamContext).
close_reason: implemented ls-remote SHA compare before fetch+pull in both doPull and pullTeamContext

title: Replace cmd/ox HTML string-concat with generator calls
description: Replace Path 2 (session_html.go) and Path 3 (session_view_html.go) HTML generation with calls to the Path 1 generator (internal/session/html). Requires converging the two different HTML templates (export template with aha moments/insights vs viewer template with embed mode/recording warning). Deferred from initial consolidation pass — regression tests (ox-cjx) are in place for safety. This is the largest remaining task in the epic.

title: Consolidate three diverged HTML generation paths into single pipeline
description:
Three separate HTML rendering pipelines have diverged, causing repeated bugs where fixes to one path don't apply to others (see ox-aql).

**Current state — three paths:**

| Path | File | Used by | Template |
|------|------|---------|----------|
| 1 | internal/session/html/ | ox session stop, ox agent <id> session stop | Embedded assets |
| 2 | cmd/ox/session_html.go | ox session export, ox session push-summary | Inline const strings |
| 3 | cmd/ox/session_view_html.go | ox session view --html | Inline const strings |

**Duplicated logic (copy-pasted, then diverged):**
- Entry type mapping (mapEntryType vs viewHTMLMapEntryType vs normalizeMessageType)
- Role-to-type mapping
- HTML escaping, output truncation
- Title generation
- Timestamp parsing (ts vs timestamp, RFC3339 vs RFC3339Nano)
- Root-level vs nested content extraction
- CSS/JS (inline strings in paths 2+3, embedded assets in path 1)

**Missing from some paths:**
- Path 1: no diff rendering, no collapsible tool messages
- Path 3: no sender labels, no summary/aha moments, no keyboard shortcuts
- Path 2: uses local openInBrowser() instead of cli.OpenInBrowser (canonical violation)
- Path 3: dead code (processContentWithMermaid — defined, never called)

**Proposed approach:**
Converge on a single rendering pipeline in internal/session/html/ that all command paths feed into, with options struct for toggling features (summary panel, sender labels, aha moments, etc.). Entry type mapping, timestamp parsing, content extraction, and CSS/JS live in one place.
notes:
## Test Coverage Audit

**Path 3 (session_view_html.go) has ZERO tests** — this is the path where all three rendering bugs in ox-aql were found. No tests for viewHTMLBuildMessageView, viewHTMLMapEntryType, viewHTMLMapRoleToType, or any of the viewHTML* functions.

**Markdown generator (internal/session/markdown.go) has ZERO tests** — the MarkdownGenerator used by session stop/export has no JSONL-in → markdown-out tests at all.

### What needs testing (minimum):

1. **viewHTMLMapEntryType** — table-driven test covering user, assistant, message, tool_call, tool_result, tool, system, unknown (this is where the bug was)
2. **viewHTMLBuildMessageView** — test with:
   - Root-level content (imported JSONL format)
   - Nested data.content (standard format)
   - ts field (imported) vs timestamp field (standard)
   - user/assistant/tool entry types at root level
3. **viewHTMLBuildTemplateData** — end-to-end: _meta header + entries → template data with correct metadata, message count, types
4. **MarkdownGenerator.Generate** — JSONL session → markdown output, verify entries appear with correct formatting
5. **Cross-format integration tests** — same JSONL input through all three HTML paths should produce equivalent content (different styling is fine, missing entries is not)

### Consolidation reduces test burden:
Once paths converge into single pipeline (the main fix), these duplicated test concerns collapse into one test suite.
close_reason: Tracked by epic ox-iz9 which has detailed subtasks. This was the initial analysis issue that spawned the epic.

title: Check write access at session start for public repo viewers
description: Issue #2: session start should fail fast if user has read-only access, instead of recording a full session and failing at upload time.
close_reason: Added checkUploadAccess() call in runAgentSessionStart() before recording begins. Returns clear error for read-only viewers.

title: Auto-recover ghost sessions on agent prime
description: Bug: When user exits Claude Code without running /ox-session-stop (e.g., after being told to restart for hooks), the .recording.json persists. On next launch with a new agent ID, ox session list shows nothing (queries ledger) but /ox-session-start fails with 'already recording' (finds orphaned local state). Fix: During ox agent prime (or at session start), check if existing .recording.json belongs to the current Claude session. If not (different agent ID or session marker), auto-recover or clear it. Key files: internal/session/recording.go (LoadRecordingState returns first .recording.json without agent ID filtering), cmd/ox/agent_session.go:90 (only checks if ANY session exists), cmd/ox/prime_session_marker.go (doesn't clean old sessions). The 24-hour stale threshold in doctor is too long for this scenario — ghost sessions from a hooks restart happen within minutes.
close_reason: Ghost sessions from different agent IDs are now auto-cleared on session start

title: session summarize should accept a session name argument
description: Currently 'ox agent <id> session summarize' only works on the active recording session. It cannot generate a summary for an imported or completed session by name. This blocks the import pipeline from producing summaries. Fix: accept an optional session name argument (e.g. 'ox agent <id> session summarize <session-name>') to generate summary.md for any session in the store.
close_reason: Added optional session name positional argument to ox agent <id> session summarize. Priority: --file > session-name > active recording > latest session.

title: Fix --force flag parsing in ox agent session abort
description: cobra intercepts --force before it reaches the abort handler's hasFlag() check. Register --force as a proper cobra flag on agentCmd, update abort to read via cobra, add regression test. Must preserve friction telemetry.
close_reason: Registered --force as cobra persistent flag on agentCmd, updated abort handler to read via cmd.Flag(), added regression test

title: Cloud doctor API endpoint returns unavailable
description: The /api/v1/repo/{repo_id}/doctor endpoint is either not deployed or returning non-200. ox doctor shows 'Cloud doctor (unavailable) - Extended cloud-side health checks could not be performed'. Need to verify the endpoint exists on the backend and returns proper responses.
close_reason: Fixed: CLI was calling /api/v1/repo/{id}/doctor but server has /api/v1/public/repos/{id}/doctor. Updated repoDoctorPath constant in internal/api/repo.go.

title: Extract shared git safety primitives into internal/gitutil
description: Issue #476: daemon has git safety guards (timeouts, lock files, rebase checks, FETCH_HEAD dedup, output sanitization) that CLI lacks. Extract into shared internal/gitutil package so both use same code paths.
close_reason: Extracted git safety primitives (lock files, rebase checks, FETCH_HEAD dedup, output sanitization, context-aware RunGit) into internal/gitutil. Daemon and CLI now share the same code paths. All tests pass, 100% coverage on new package.

title: session stop returns nonexistent raw_path
description: ox agent session stop returned raw_path pointing to 2026-02-18T13-13-ryan-OxzZZH but the actual cache directory is 2026-02-18T12-45-user-OxzZZH. The session name in the JSON output doesn't match the filesystem. This causes summary generation to fail because the raw.jsonl file can't be found at the returned path.
close_reason: fixed: stop now reuses session name from start instead of regenerating, and start now passes username
